title: Washington and Pharma Are Helping China Win
summary: Former Democratic Party Senator from Michigan, Debbie Stabenow, warns that the PRC is running its familiar economic aggression playbook to dominate pharmaceutical development as it did in other sectors. She urges the FDA to stop accepting uninspected Chinese trial data and Congress to add branded pharmaceuticals to investment restrictions before America's access to medicines becomes Beijing's next chokepoint.

title: Those Anti-Indian Posts and the “Great Replacement” Theory
summary: The Dean of RSIS in Singapore writes that a campaign of PRC-linked online posts claiming Indians were "overrunning" Chinese-majority Singapore exemplify how the Great Replacement conspiracy theory, which has fueled extremism in other parts of the world, is being adapted to local contexts. He observes that Singapore must defend its multicultural social cohesion against such hostile information campaigns through law,...

title: China Is Abusing AI: Chatbots are deftly disseminating Beijing’s talking points.
summary: While attention focuses on which country will dominate AI development, the Chinese Communist Party is already exploiting AI tools to run covert propaganda campaigns, sharpen domestic censorship, and skew chatbot outputs toward the Party's narratives, turning a technology once hoped to democratize information into an instrument of authoritarian influence.

title: Russia approved secret China military training at top level, sources say
summary: According to European officials and documents seen by Reuters, the PRC conducted covert training of Russian forces, which was personally approved by Russia's defense minister. It involved at least four Russian and Chinese generals and covered nuclear, biological, and chemical weapons. This signals that strategically significant military cooperation between Beijing and Moscow is accelerating despite denials by...

title: How longtime GOP Senator Lindsey Graham went from critic to ally of President Trump
summary: Longtime Republican Sen. Lindsey Graham has died at the age of 71. The foreign policy hawk and former critic-turned-ally of President Trump was running for re-election in South Carolina.

title: Politics chat: Lindsey Graham dies at 71, Congress back in session, U.S. strikes Iran
summary: South Carolina Senator Lindsey Graham's sudden death at 71 has shocked Washington. Once a fierce critic of then-candidate Donald Trump, he became one of the president's most consistent defenders.

title: Sen. Lindsey Graham of South Carolina, Trump ally and foreign policy hawk, dies at 71

title: From staunch critic to fierce ally: Graham's long, strange and consequential friendship with Trump
summary: After the Capitol riot on Jan. 6, 2021, Sen. Lindsey Graham said he had finally had enough of the man who was championed by the mob that stormed the pillar of American democracy: President Donald Trump.

title: Sen. Lindsey Graham dies after a 'brief and sudden illness,' his office says

title: Title IX’s Failed Experiment: Why Accommodating Sex Differences Beats Engineered Parity
summary: Introduction In 2024, a Tunisian man defeated a Chinese woman for the Olympic women’s boxing gold medal. title: Can LLM Agents Select and Engage with Biological Tools?
